metadata.title: B1 Preliminary Gold Mock 1
metadata.description: M3.1 academic reference mock for B1 Preliminary — reading, writing, listening, and speaking full form.
metadata.authoringNotes: M4.1 Gold Mock — manually authored and academically reviewed.

parts.title: Part 1 — Listen and choose the correct picture
parts.instructions: Part 1 — Listen and choose the correct picture

audio.transcript: Man: Could I have the chicken sandwich and a glass of water, please? Waiter: Of course. I'll bring it to your table.
audio.caption: Part 1 — Listen and choose the correct picture

parts.title: Part 2 — Listen and choose the best answer
parts.instructions: Part 2 — Listen and choose the best answer

audio.transcript: Girl: I missed chemistry because I had a dentist appointment. Teacher: Remember to collect the notes from your partner.
audio.caption: Part 2 — Listen and choose the best answer

parts.title: Part 3 — Listen and complete each gap
parts.instructions: Part 3 — Listen and complete each gap

audio.transcript: Organiser: The conference is at the city hall. Adult tickets cost fifteen pounds. The weather forecast says it will rain later.
audio.caption: Part 3 — Listen and complete each gap

audio.transcript: Speaker: My first solo trip was to Portugal. I love live theatre because every performance feels different.
audio.caption: Part 4 — Listen to the monologue

parts.title: Part 5 — Listen and match each speaker to an activity
parts.instructions: Part 5 — Listen and match each speaker to an activity

audio.transcript: Five students describe clubs: film club, climbing wall, pottery class, robotics team, and bird sanctuary volunteering.
audio.caption: Part 5 — Listen and match each speaker to an activity

questions.questionText: Parking for permit holders only — all other vehicles will be removed.

choices.text: Anyone may park here for one hour.
Only drivers with a permit can leave their car here.

choices.text: You can collect urgent medicine at the hospital.

choices.text: Visitors cannot enter after half past four.

choices.text: Students can ask for a printed copy if they want one.

choices.text: Customers must bring or buy a cup they can use again.

Paper cups are free at the counter.

pairs.rightText: is training to be a nurse

questions.questionText: Why did Maya join the photography society?

content.passage: When Maya moved to a new city for university, she felt nervous about making friends. During the first week, she joined a photography society because she loved taking pictures of street life. At the first meeting, members shared tips about lighting and composition. Maya volunteered to photograph the society's charity exhibition, which raised money for a local hospital. Working on the project helped her meet students from different courses. By the end of the term, she had not only improved her technical skills but also built a close group of friends who still travel together during holidays.

choices.text: Because she enjoyed photographing street life
Because she wanted to work in a hospital

questions.questionText: Gap (1): fresh produce ___ expensive

content.passage: Urban gardening has become popular among young professionals who live in flats. Many people grow herbs and vegetables on balconies because fresh produce (1) ___ expensive in supermarkets. Community gardens also offer shared spaces where neighbours can (2) ___ advice and tools. Experts recommend starting with easy plants such as lettuce or mint, which grow quickly and need little (3) ___. Some cities provide free workshops so residents learn how to compost and save (4) ___. Although space is limited, careful planning can turn a small corner into a productive and (5) ___ garden. In the long term, gardening can reduce stress and encourage people to spend more time (6) ___.

questions.questionText: Which sentence best fits gap 1? (after the opening sentence about navigation)

content.passage: [A] For centuries, people have looked to the night sky for navigation and inspiration. [B] Today, light pollution in cities makes it harder to see stars clearly. [C] However, dark-sky parks protect areas where astronomers and tourists can observe constellations. [D] These parks also support wildlife that depends on natural darkness. [E] As a result, several countries have introduced laws to reduce unnecessary outdoor lighting. [F] Visiting a dark-sky park can be an unforgettable experience for families and students alike.

choices.text: [B] Today, light pollution in cities makes it harder to see stars clearly.
[F] Visiting a dark-sky park can be an unforgettable experience for families and students alike.
[D] These parks also support wildlife that depends on natural darkness.

questions.questionText: Write an email in response to your English teacher.

content.prompt: Your English teacher has sent you an email about a project on local history. Write an email to your teacher.
content.taskDescription: Write about 100 words. Answer all three points.
requiredPoints: Explain which historical place you want to research
Ask for advice about useful sources

questions.questionText: Write a story that begins with the sentence below.

content.prompt: Your teacher has asked you to write a story. Your story must begin with this sentence: 'The train was late, but I didn't mind.'

Which speaker describes a pottery class?

Answer the examiner's questions about your life and interests.

content.prompt: The examiner will ask about your daily routine, studies, and what you like doing in your free time.
followUpQuestions: Tell me about the subjects you enjoy at school.
What would you like to do after you finish school?
How do you usually relax after a busy day?

questions.questionText: Describe the photograph and say what you think is happening.

content.prompt: Look at the photograph. Describe the place and the people, and explain why they might be there.

questions.questionText: Discuss ways to make your neighbourhood more environmentally friendly.

content.prompt: Talk with your partner about things people could do to protect the environment in your area.
followUpQuestions: Which idea would be easiest to start?
How could young people encourage others to take part?
